sentences.zh: 光祿大夫孔光、左將軍公孫祿、右將軍王安、光祿勳馬宮、光祿大夫龔勝劾嘉迷國罔上不道，請與廷尉雜治。

translations.literal: Grand Counselor Kong Guang, Left General Gongsun Lu, Right General Wang An, Superintendent of the Household Ma Gong, and Grand Counselor Gong Sheng impeached Jia for deluding the state, deceiving the superior, and lacking the Way, requesting jointly to try with the Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Guang, Gongsun Lu, Wang An, Ma Gong, and Gong Sheng impeached Jia for misleading the state and deceiving the throne, and asked that he be handed to the commandant of justice for joint trial.

sentences.zh: 勝獨以為嘉備宰相，諸事並廢，咎由嘉生；

translations.literal: Sheng alone thought that Jia filled the chancellor's place; the various affairs together were abandoned; fault arose from Jia;
translations.idiomatic: Gong Sheng alone argued that as chancellor Jia had let every duty slide—the blame lay with him;

sentences.zh: 嘉坐薦相等，微薄，以應迷國罔上不道，恐不可以示天下。

translations.literal: Jia by sitting in recommending Xiang and the others was slight and thin; to match deluding the state, deceiving the superior, and lacking the Way—he feared it could not be shown to all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: yet his offense in recommending Liang Xiang was a small matter—too thin a reed on which to hang the capital charge of deceiving the realm; it would not convince the empire.

sentences.zh: 遂可光等奏。

translations.literal: Thereupon he approved Guang and the others' memorial.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved Kong Guang's memorial.

sentences.zh: 光等請謁者召嘉詣廷尉詔獄，制曰：「票騎將軍、御史大夫、中二千石、二千石、諸大夫、博士、議郎議。」

translations.literal: Guang and the others requested the Herald Summoner summon Jia to the Commandant of Justice's edict prison; the edict said: "General of Agile Cavalry, Imperial Counselor, those of two-thousand-dan and middle two-thousand-dan, the various grandees, erudites, and Gentlemen Consultants—deliberate."
translations.idiomatic: Kong Guang and his party asked that a herald escort Jia to the imperial prison under the commandant of justice. The edict read: "Let the general of agile cavalry, the imperial counselor, all two-thousand-dan officials, the grandees, academicians, and consultants deliberate."

sentences.zh: 衛尉雲等五十人以為「

translations.literal: Commandant of the Guards Yun and fifty others thought "
translations.idiomatic: Commandant of the guards Wang Yun and fifty colleagues held that "

sentences.zh: 如光等言可許」。

translations.literal: as Guang and the others said, it can be permitted."
translations.idiomatic: Kong Guang's proposal should be accepted."

sentences.zh: 議郎龔等以為「嘉言事前後相違，無所執守，不任宰相之職，宜奪爵土，免為庶人。」

translations.literal: Gentlemen Consultants Gong and others thought: "Jia's words and affairs front and back contradict each other; he has nothing he holds to; he is not fit for the chancellor's post; it is fitting to strip fief and soil and dismiss him to be a commoner."
translations.idiomatic: Consultants led by Gong argued that Jia's statements contradicted themselves, that he lacked a consistent standard, that he was unfit to be chancellor, and that he should be stripped of rank and fief and reduced to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 永信少府猛等十人以為「聖王斷獄，必先原心定罪，探意立情，故死者不抱恨而入地，生者不銜怨而受罪。

translations.literal: Privy Treasurer of Yongxin Meng and ten others thought: "When sage kings judge cases, they always first trace the heart to fix guilt, probe intent to establish circumstance; therefore the dead do not embrace resentment entering the earth, and the living do not harbor resentment while receiving punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Privy treasurer Meng of Yongxin and nine others countered: "A sage king weighs a man's intent before fixing guilt, so the dead go to the grave unresentful and the living accept punishment without bitterness.

sentences.zh: 明主躬聖德，重大臣刑辟，廣延有司議，欲使海內咸服。

translations.literal: An enlightened lord personally embodies sage virtue, heavily treats great ministers' punishments, broadly extends to the responsible offices for deliberation, wishing to make within the seas all submit.
translations.idiomatic: A wise ruler, himself steeped in virtue, treats a minister's punishment as a grave matter and casts the net wide for counsel, so that the whole realm may be persuaded of the justice done.

sentences.zh: 嘉罪名雖應法，聖王之於大臣，在輿為下，御坐則起，疾病視之無數，死則臨弔之，廢宗廟之祭，進之以禮，退之以義，誄之以行。

translations.literal: Although Jia's crime name matches the law, the sage king's toward great ministers: in the carriage he is below; at the seated session he rises; in sickness he visits them without number; at death he comes to mourn; he suspends the ancestral temple's sacrifices; advancing them by ritual, retiring them by righteousness, eulogizing them by conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Even if Jia's offense fits the statute, sage kings treat high ministers with ceremony: they bow lower in the carriage, rise when the minister is seated, visit him tirelessly in illness, mourn him at death, and pause the ancestral rites; they advance him with ritual, dismiss him with justice, and praise his conduct in the funeral ode.

sentences.zh: 案嘉本以相等為罪，罪惡雖著，大臣括髮關械、裸躬就笞，非所以重國褒宗廟也。

translations.literal: Investigating Jia's root, he took Xiang and the others as crime; though guilt and evil are manifest, for great ministers to bind hair and wear fetters, bare body and receive beating is not the means to honor the state and exalt the ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: Jia's real offense was recommending Liang Xiang; however grave that looks, to shackle a chief minister, strip him, and flog him is no way to dignify the state or bring honor to the imperial shrines.

sentences.zh: 今春月寒氣錯繆，霜露數降，宜示天下以寬和。

translations.literal: Now in spring month cold vapor is wrong and twisted; frost and dew repeatedly descend; it is fitting to show all under Heaven with breadth and harmony.
translations.idiomatic: This spring the weather runs wild—frost and dew keep falling out of season; it is time to show the empire mercy and restraint.

sentences.zh: 臣等不知大義，唯陛下察焉。」

translations.literal: Your servants do not know the great righteousness; only may Your Majesty examine it."
translations.idiomatic: We may lack the larger view—may Your Majesty judge."

sentences.zh: 有詔假謁者節，召丞相詣廷尉詔獄。

translations.literal: There was an edict lending the Herald his staff of authority, summoning the Chancellor to go to the Commandant of Justice's edict prison.
translations.idiomatic: An edict followed: lend the herald's baton of office and escort the chancellor to the commandant of justice's imperial prison.

sentences.zh: 使者既到府，掾史涕泣，共和藥進嘉，嘉不肯服。

translations.literal: When the envoy had reached the bureau, aides and clerics wept tears, together harmonized poison and presented to Jia; Jia was unwilling to take it.
translations.idiomatic: When the herald reached the chancellery, the staff wept and mixed poison for Jia to drink; he refused.

sentences.zh: 主簿曰：「將相不對理陳冤，相踵以為故事，君侯宜引決。」

translations.literal: The chief clerk said: "Generals and ministers do not go to court to plead their innocence; one after another they have made that the established precedent; my lord ought to take his own life."
translations.idiomatic: The chief clerk said, "Generals and ministers do not submit to public trial to plead innocence—it has become the custom to take one's own life; you should follow suit, my lord."

sentences.zh: 使者危坐府門上。

translations.literal: The envoy sat upright on the bureau gate.
translations.idiomatic: The herald sat stiffly atop the chancellery gate.

sentences.zh: 主簿復前進藥，嘉引藥杯以擊地，謂官屬曰：「丞相幸得備位三公，奉職負國，當伏刑都市以示萬眾。

translations.literal: The chief clerk again advanced with the poison; Jia drew the medicine cup to strike the ground and said to his officials: "The Chancellor has fortunately obtained to fill a place among the Three Dukes; he bears office and fails the state; he ought to submit to execution in the marketplace to show the myriad crowds.
translations.idiomatic: The chief clerk pressed the cup again; Jia dashed it to the floor and told his officers, "I have had the honor to rank among the Three Excellencies; I held office and failed the state—I should die in the public market as a lesson to the multitude.

sentences.zh: 丞相豈兒女子邪，何謂咀藥而死！」

translations.literal: Is the Chancellor a boy or girl—how speak of chewing medicine and dying!"
translations.idiomatic: Do you take the chancellor for some weak girl who ends her life with poison?"

sentences.zh: 嘉遂裝出，見使者再拜受詔，乘吏小車，去蓋不冠，隨使者詣廷尉。

translations.literal: Jia thereupon dressed and went out, saw the envoy, twice bowed and received the edict, rode a clerk's small cart, removed the canopy and did not cap, followed the envoy to the Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: Jia then dressed, came out, bowed twice to the herald and accepted the edict, rode a clerk's plain cart without canopy or cap, and followed him to the commandant of justice.

sentences.zh: 廷尉收嘉丞相新甫侯印綬，縛嘉載致都船詔獄。

translations.literal: The Commandant of Justice seized Jia's Chancellor Marquis of Xinfu seals and ribbons, bound Jia, loaded and delivered him to the Capital Ships edict prison.
translations.idiomatic: The commandant took Jia's seals as chancellor and marquis of Xinfu, bound him, and sent him in a cart to the imperial prison under the superintendent of capital ships.

sentences.zh: 上聞嘉生自詣吏，大怒，使將軍以下與五二千石雜治。

translations.literal: When the sovereign heard Jia alive had gone himself to the clerks, he was greatly angry and caused from generals downward together with five two-thousand-dan jointly to try him.
translations.idiomatic: Learning that Jia had surrendered alive, the emperor flew into a rage and ordered generals and five senior officials to sit in joint judgment on him.

sentences.zh: 吏詰問嘉，嘉對曰：「案事者思得實。

translations.literal: The clerks questioned Jia; Jia replied: "Those who investigate cases think to obtain the real.
translations.idiomatic: The examiners pressed him; Jia answered, "Anyone who handles a case should seek the truth.

sentences.zh: 竊見相等前治東平王獄，不以雲為不當死，欲關公卿示重慎；

translations.literal: Your servant privately saw that Xiang and the others formerly tried the King of Dongping's case; they did not deem Yun ought not die; they wished to pass it to dukes and ministers to show gravity and care;
translations.idiomatic: Liang Xiang and his colleagues never said King Yun should escape death—they only wanted the high ministers to review the file and show due care.

sentences.zh: 置驛馬傳囚，勢不得踰冬月，誠不見其外內顧望阿附為雲驗。

translations.literal: They set relay horses to transmit the prisoner; by circumstance they could not pass winter month; truly one does not see their outward-inward looking and fawning attachment as verification for Yun.
translations.idiomatic: They used post horses to move the prisoner so the case could not slip past the winter session; I see no proof that they were stalling or currying favor for Yun.

sentences.zh: 復幸得蒙大赦，相等皆良善吏，臣竊為國惜賢，不私此三人。」

translations.literal: Again fortunately they received the great amnesty; Xiang and the others were all good and kind clerks; your servant secretly for the state pities worthies; he does not privately favor these three men."
translations.idiomatic: They were later swept clean by a general amnesty; they were honest officials all, and your servant grieved for the state's loss of talent—not from any private fondness for those three."

sentences.zh: 獄吏曰：「苟如此，則君何以為罪猶當？

translations.literal: The prison clerk said: "If it is thus, then with what does my lord still have guilt that fits?
translations.idiomatic: The jailer said, "If that is so, what charge then still hangs on you?

sentences.zh: 有以負國，不空入獄矣。」

translations.literal: There is something whereby you failed the state; it is not for nothing you entered prison."
translations.idiomatic: You must have failed the state in some other way—you did not land here for nothing."

sentences.zh: 吏稍侵辱嘉，嘉喟然卬天嘆曰：「幸得充備宰相，不能進賢退不肖，以是負國，死有餘責。」

translations.literal: The clerks gradually encroached and insulted Jia; Jia sighed long, looked up to Heaven, and sighed: "Fortunately I obtained to fill out the chancellor's place; I could not advance the worthy and retire the unworthy; by this I failed the state; in death there is surplus blame."
translations.idiomatic: As the clerks grew rougher, Jia looked up and groaned, "I was honored with the chancellorship yet failed to promote the good and remove the wicked—for that I betrayed the state, and death cannot expiate my fault."

sentences.zh: 吏問賢不肖主名，嘉曰：「賢，故丞相孔光、故大司空何武，不能進；

translations.literal: The clerks asked the names of worthy and unworthy principals; Jia said: "Worthy: the former Chancellor Kong Guang, the former Grand Minister of Works He Wu—I could not advance them;
translations.idiomatic: They asked him to name the worthy and the base. He said, "The worthy: former Chancellor Kong Guang and former Grand Minister of Works He Wu—I could not bring them forward;

sentences.zh: 惡，高安侯董賢父子，佞邪亂朝，而不能退。

translations.literal: Evil: Marquis of Gao'an Dong Xian father and son, sycophantic and deviant, disordering the court—yet I could not retire them.
translations.idiomatic: the base: Marquis of Gao'an Dong Xian and his father, sycophants who threw the court into chaos—yet I could not remove them.

sentences.zh: 罪當死，死無所恨。」

translations.literal: Guilt fits death; in death there is nothing to resent."
translations.idiomatic: I deserve death for it, and I die without complaint."

sentences.zh: 嘉繫獄二十餘日，不食歐血而死。

translations.literal: Jia was bound in prison over twenty days, did not eat, vomited blood and died.
translations.idiomatic: After more than twenty days in prison without food, he vomited blood and died.

sentences.zh: 帝舅大司馬票騎將軍丁明素重嘉而憐之，上遂免明，以董賢代之，語在賢傳。

translations.literal: The emperor's maternal uncle Grand Marshal General of Agile Cavalry Ding Ming always esteemed Jia and pitied him; the sovereign thereupon dismissed Ming and replaced him with Dong Xian—the words are in Xian's biography.
translations.idiomatic: Ding Ming, the emperor's uncle, grand marshal and general of agile cavalry, had always respected Jia and pitied his fate; the emperor removed Ding and gave his post to Dong Xian, as told in Dong Xian's memoir.

sentences.zh: 嘉為相三年誅，國除。

translations.literal: Jia as chancellor in the third year was executed; the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Jia had been chancellor three years when he was put to death and his marquisate struck off.

sentences.zh: 死後上覽其對而思嘉言，復以孔光代嘉為丞相，徵用何武為御史大夫。

translations.literal: After death the sovereign read his responses and recalled Jia's words; again he made Kong Guang replace Jia as Chancellor and summoned He Wu to be Imperial Counselor.
translations.idiomatic: After his death the emperor read Jia's prison statements, remembered his counsel, appointed Kong Guang chancellor in his place, and recalled He Wu as imperial counselor.

sentences.zh: 元始四年，詔書追錄忠臣，封嘉子崇為新甫侯，追諡嘉為忠侯。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yuanshi, an edict document posthumously recorded loyal ministers, enfeoffed Jia's son Chong as Marquis of Xinfu, and posthumously gave Jia the title Marquis Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshi year four an edict honored loyal ministers posthumously: Jia's son Chong received the marquisate of Xinfu, and Jia himself was titled posthumously Marquis of Loyalty.

sentences.zh: 師丹字仲公，琅邪東武人也。

translations.literal: Shi Dan, styled Zhonggong, was a man of Dongwu in Langye.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Dan, courtesy name Zhonggong, was a native of Dongwu in Langye commandery.

sentences.zh: 治詩，事匡衡。

translations.literal: He studied the Odes and served Kuang Heng.
translations.idiomatic: He studied the Odes under Kuang Heng.

sentences.zh: 舉孝廉為郎。

translations.literal: Recommended Filial and Incorrupt he became a Gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filial and incorrupt, he was appointed a court gentleman.

sentences.zh: 元帝末，為博士，免。

translations.literal: At the end of Emperor Yuan he was an Erudite; he was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Late in Emperor Yuan's reign he held an academician's chair, then lost the post.

sentences.zh: 建始中，州舉茂材，復補博士，出為東平王太傅。

translations.literal: In the Jianshi period the province recommended him as Abundant Talent; again he was supplemented as Erudite; he went out to be Grand Tutor to the King of Dongping.
translations.idiomatic: During Jianshi the province nominated him as abundantly talented; he was reappointed academician and then became grand tutor to the king of Dongping.

sentences.zh: 丞相方進、御史大夫孔光舉丹論議深博，廉正守道，徵入為光祿大夫、丞相司直。

translations.literal: Chancellor Fangjin and Imperial Counselor Kong Guang recommended Dan as deep and broad in disputation, incorrupt and straight, guarding the Way; he was summoned in to be Grand Counselor of the Palace and Chancellor's Director of Integrity.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Zhai Fangjin and Imperial Counselor Kong Guang cited Dan's learning, integrity, and moral steadfastness; he was recalled as grand counselor of the palace and director of integrity for the chancellor.

sentences.zh: 數月，復以光祿大夫給事中，由是為少府、光祿勳、侍中，甚見尊重。

translations.literal: After several months, again as Grand Counselor of the Palace he was given Palace Attendance; from this he became Privy Treasurer, Superintendent of the Household, and Palace Attendant; he was greatly seen with respect.
translations.idiomatic: Months later he added palace advisory duty, then rose through privy treasurer, superintendent of the household, and palace attendant—each step bringing him greater esteem.

sentences.zh: 成帝末年，立定陶王為皇太子，以丹為太子太傅。

translations.literal: At the end of Emperor Cheng, they established the King of Dingtao as heir-apparent and made Dan Grand Tutor to the Heir-apparent.
translations.idiomatic: Near the end of Emperor Cheng's reign the king of Dingtao was named crown prince, and Dan became his grand tutor.

sentences.zh: 哀帝即位，為左將軍，賜爵關內侯，食邑，領尚書事，遂代王莽為大司馬，封高樂侯。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ai ascended, he became Left General, was granted rank Marquis Within the Passes, with fief income, headed Masters of Writing affairs, then replaced Wang Mang as Grand Marshal and was enfeoffed Marquis of Gaole.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ai made him left general, ennobled him as marquis within the passes with a stipendiary estate, put him in charge of the secretariat, then had him succeed Wang Mang as grand marshal with the title marquis of Gaole.

sentences.zh: 月餘，徙為大司空。

translations.literal: After more than a month he was transferred to be Grand Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: A little over a month later he was moved to grand minister of works.

sentences.zh: 會有上書言古者以龜貝為貨，今以錢易之，民以故貧，宜可改幣。

translations.literal: It happened that someone submitted a memorial saying that in antiquity they used turtle and shell as goods; now they have exchanged them for cash; the people for this reason are poor; it is fitting to change the currency.
translations.idiomatic: Someone memorialized that antiquity used cowries and shells as money, whereas today metal coin has made the people poor, and that the currency system ought to be reformed.

sentences.zh: 上以問丹，丹對言可改。

translations.literal: The sovereign thereupon asked Dan; Dan replied saying it could be changed.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked Dan, who answered that reform was feasible.

sentences.zh: 章下有司議，皆以為行錢以來久，難卒變易。

translations.literal: The document was sent down to the responsible offices for deliberation; all deemed that since employing cash it has been long; it is hard suddenly to change.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial went to the ministries for debate; all agreed that cash had been in use too long to be overturned overnight.

sentences.zh: 丹老人，忘其前語，後從公卿議。

translations.literal: Dan was an old man; he forgot his former words; afterward he followed the dukes and ministers' deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: Dan was elderly; he forgot his earlier opinion and later sided with the majority of the high ministers.

sentences.zh: 又丹使吏書奏，吏私寫其草，丁、傅子弟聞之，使人上書告丹上封事行道人遍持其書。

translations.literal: Also Dan caused a clerk to write the memorial; the clerk privately copied its draft; the Ding and Fu younger kin heard it and caused someone to submit a memorial accusing Dan of, when submitting a sealed memorial, having travelers on the road everywhere hold his document.
translations.idiomatic: Dan had a clerk draft a memorial; the clerk secretly copied the rough text. Young men of the Ding and Fu factions heard of it and lodged a charge that Dan had let his sealed memorial circulate among travelers on the highway.

sentences.zh: 上以問將軍中朝臣，皆對曰：「忠臣不顯諫，大臣奏事不宜漏泄，令吏民傳寫流聞四方。

translations.literal: The sovereign thereupon asked generals and inner-court ministers; all replied: "A loyal minister does not display remonstrance; a great minister's memorial affairs ought not leak; causing clerks and commoners to copy and transmit so it flows and is heard in the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor put the question to generals and inner-court ministers, who replied, "A loyal minister does not publish his remonstrance abroad; a chief minister's papers must not leak and be copied by officials and commoners until rumor spreads everywhere.

sentences.zh: 『臣不密則失身』，宜下廷尉治。」

translations.literal: 'If the minister is not secretive then he loses his person'—it is fitting to send down to the Commandant of Justice to treat."
translations.idiomatic: 'When a minister fails to keep secrets, he loses his life'—the case should go to the commandant of justice."

sentences.zh: 事下廷尉，廷尉劾丹大不敬。

translations.literal: The matter was sent down to the Commandant of Justice; the Commandant of Justice impeached Dan for great irreverence.
translations.idiomatic: The matter went to the commandant of justice, who impeached Dan for grave disrespect.

sentences.zh: 事未決，給事中博士申咸、炔欽上書，言「丹經行無比，自近世大臣能若丹者少。

translations.literal: Before the matter was decided, Palace Attendant Erudites Shen Xian and Geng Qin submitted a memorial, saying: "Dan's classical conduct has no peer; among great ministers of recent generations few can match Dan.
translations.idiomatic: Before judgment fell, Palace Attendants Shen Xian and Geng Qin submitted that no minister of recent times matched Dan's learning and conduct.

sentences.zh: 發憤懣，奏封事，不及深思遠慮，使主簿書，漏泄之過不在丹。

translations.literal: Venting indignation and resentment, he submitted a sealed memorial; he did not reach deep thought and far planning; he caused the chief clerk to write; the fault of leak does not lie on Dan.
translations.idiomatic: He spoke in honest anger in a sealed memorial, without weighing every consequence, and had his chief clerk fair-copy it—the leak was not his doing.

sentences.zh: 以此貶黜，恐不厭眾心。」

translations.literal: By this to demote and dismiss—your servants fear it will not satisfy the multitude's hearts."
translations.idiomatic: To dismiss him on that ground would not satisfy public opinion."

sentences.zh: 尚書劾咸、欽：「幸得以儒官選擢備腹心，上所折中定疑，知丹社稷重臣，議罪處罰，國之所慎，咸、欽初傅經義以為當治，事以暴列，乃復上書妄稱譽丹，前後相違，不敬。」

translations.literal: The Masters of Writing impeached Xian and Qin: "They fortunately obtained by Ru office selection and promotion to fill the inner core; on what the sovereign balances and fixes doubt; they knew Dan was a heavy minister of the altars of soil and grain; deliberating guilt and applying punishment is what the state is careful of; Xian and Qin at first attached classical meaning deeming it ought to be tried; when the affair was already exposed and arrayed, they again submitted a memorial recklessly praising Dan—front and back contradicting—irreverence."
translations.idiomatic: The secretariat impeached Shen and Geng: "Raised from scholarly posts to advise the throne, they knew Dan was a pillar of the state and that fixing his punishment was a matter of national gravity. They first cited the classics to argue he should be prosecuted; once the facts were public, they reversed themselves with a memorial praising him—contradictory and disrespectful."

sentences.zh: 上貶咸、欽秩各二等，遂策免丹曰：「夫三公者，朕之腹心也，輔善相過，匡率百僚，和合天下者也。

translations.literal: The sovereign demoted Xian and Qin's rank each two grades; thereupon by edict he dismissed Dan, saying: "As for the Three Dukes, they are Our inner core; they assist good and correct faults, rectify and lead the hundred officials, harmonize and unite all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor docked Shen and Geng two ranks each, then issued an edict dismissing Dan: "The Three Excellencies are my inner council: they nurture what is right, correct my faults, give heart to the bureaucracy, and bring harmony to the realm.

sentences.zh: 朕既不明，委政於公，間者陰陽不調，寒暑失常，變異婁臻，山崩地震，河決泉涌，流殺人民，百姓流連，無所歸心，司空之職尤廢焉。

translations.literal: We are already not enlightened; We entrusted government to you; recently yin and yang have not been tuned; cold and heat have lost their norm; portents repeatedly arrive; mountains collapsed and earth quaked; rivers burst and springs gushed; flowing they killed the people; the hundred surnames lingered and drifted, having nowhere to fix their hearts—the Grand Minister of Works' duty especially has been abandoned.
translations.idiomatic: I am no sage; I laid the government on you—yet yin and yang have fallen out of tune, the seasons run wild, omens pile up, the earth shakes, rivers break their banks and drown the people, and the common folk are adrift with nowhere to turn. You have especially neglected the office of grand minister of works.

sentences.zh: 君在位出入三年，未聞忠言嘉謀，而反有朋黨相進不公之名。

translations.literal: In the three years since you took and left office, We have not heard loyal words or fine plans; on the contrary there is the name of faction cliques mutually advancing unfairness.
translations.idiomatic: In the three years of your tenure We have heard neither loyal counsel nor sound strategy—only whispers that you pack your faction and hand out unfair favors.

sentences.zh: 乃者以挺力田議改幣章示君，君內為朕建可改不疑；

translations.literal: Recently by Ting Litian's memorial on changing currency We showed you the document; inwardly you for Us established that it could be changed without doubt;
translations.idiomatic: When the memorial on currency reform was laid before you (the transmitted text of the sponsor's name appears corrupt), you assured Me in private without hesitation that reform was possible;

sentences.zh: 以君之言博考朝臣，君乃希眾雷同，外以為不便，令觀聽者歸非於朕。

translations.literal: Taking your words We broadly examined court ministers; you then hoped in the multitude's same echo, outwardly deeming it inconvenient, causing those who look and listen to return blame to Us.
translations.idiomatic: yet when We canvassed the court on your advice, you echoed the crowd and declared the change impractical, so that every onlooker lays the blame on Me.

sentences.zh: 朕隱忍不宣，為君受愆。

translations.literal: We secretly bore it and did not publish, for you receiving fault.
translations.idiomatic: I swallowed the affront and said nothing—I took the disgrace for you.

sentences.zh: 朕疾夫比周之徒虛偽壞化，寖以成俗，故屢以書飭君，幾君省過求己，而反不受，退有後言。

translations.literal: We hate those who factionally band together, whose falseness ruins transformation, gradually becoming custom; therefore repeatedly We used documents to admonish you, almost causing you to examine faults and seek in yourself; yet on the contrary you did not accept; retiring you had after-words.
translations.idiomatic: I detest cliques whose hypocrisy rots public morals until it becomes habit; I wrote you again and again, hoping you would search your own faults—yet you brushed it off and carped behind My back.

sentences.zh: 及君奏封事，傳於道路，布聞朝市，言事者以為大臣不忠，辜陷重辟，獲虛采名，謗譏匈匈，流於四方。

translations.literal: When you submitted a sealed memorial, it transmitted on the roads, spread and was heard in court and market; those who spoke on affairs deemed great ministers disloyal, guiltlessly trapped in heavy execution, obtaining empty borrowed fame; slander and ridicule clamored, flowing to the four quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Then your sealed memorial ran along the highways and through the markets, and critics began to say that ministers feign loyalty, wriggle out of capital charges, and steal empty fame—slander now roars in every quarter.

sentences.zh: 腹心如此，謂疏者何？

translations.literal: If the inner core is thus, what shall We say of the distant?
translations.idiomatic: If my closest ministers behave so, what may I expect of those farther out?

sentences.zh: 殆謬於二人同心之利焉，將何以率示群下，附親遠方？

translations.literal: Probably erring from the benefit of two men with one mind—how then shall We lead and show the host below, draw near kin and attach the distant?
translations.idiomatic: You have missed entirely the good of 'two minds with one purpose'—how then am I to guide the officials below or win the allegiance of the distant regions?

sentences.zh: 朕惟君位尊任重，慮不周密，懷諼迷國，進退違命，反覆異言，甚為君恥之，非所以共承天地，永保國家之意。

translations.literal: We consider that you, position honored and burden heavy, thought not comprehensively and dense; harboring deceit you deluded the state; advancing and retiring you disobeyed commands; turning and reversing you had different words—We greatly for you are ashamed of it; it is not the intent whereby jointly to receive Heaven and Earth and eternally preserve the state.
translations.idiomatic: You held high office yet schemed carelessly, harbored deceit that misled the state, defied My orders, and contradicted yourself at every turn—I am deeply shamed for you. That is no way to serve Heaven and Earth or to keep the dynasty secure.

sentences.zh: 以君嘗託傅位，未忍考於理，已詔有司赦君勿治。

translations.literal: Because you once were entrusted with the tutor's place, We could not bear to examine you by principle; We have already ordered the responsible offices to pardon you and not try you.
translations.idiomatic: Because you once served as crown tutor, I cannot bring Myself to press the full rigor of the law; I have ordered the ministries to pardon you from prosecution.

sentences.zh: 其上大司空高樂侯印綬，罷歸。」

translations.literal: Submit your Grand Minister of Works Marquis of Gaole seals and ribbons; be dismissed and return."
translations.idiomatic: Surrender the seals of grand minister of works and marquis of Gaole and retire to your home."

sentences.zh: 尚書令唐林上疏曰：「竊見免大司空丹策書，泰深痛切，君子作文，為賢者諱。

translations.literal: Masters of Writing Director Tang Lin submitted a memorial, saying: "Your servant has seen the edict document dismissing Grand Minister of Works Dan; it is too deep, painful, and cutting; the gentleman composing text should for the worthy conceal faults.
translations.idiomatic: Director of the secretariat Tang Lin wrote: "The edict dismissing Grand Minister of Works Dan is unbearably harsh; a gentleman drafting state papers should veil the faults of the worthy."

sentences.zh: 丹經為世儒宗，德為國黃耇，親傅聖躬，位在三公，所坐者微，海內未見其大過，事既已往，免爵大重，京師識者咸以為宜復丹邑爵，使奉朝請，四方所瞻卬也。

translations.literal: Dan's classics made him the generation's Ru patriarch; his virtue made him the state's hoary elder; he personally tutored the sage person; he stood among the Three Dukes; what he sat in guilt for was slight; within the seas none has seen his great fault; the affair being already past, stripping fief is too heavy; the capital's knowing ones all deem it fitting to restore Dan's fief and rank, causing him to attend court on summons—this is what the four quarters look up to.
translations.idiomatic: Dan is the foremost scholar of the age, a venerable pillar of the state, once tutor to Your Majesty's own person and a member of the Three Excellencies. His offense was trifling; the empire sees no grave crime in him. To strip his title now is excessive. Men of judgment in the capital urge that his rank and income be restored and that he attend court on summons—the eyes of the realm are on this.

sentences.zh: 惟陛下財覽眾心，有以尉復師傅之臣。」

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty judge and heed the multitude's hearts; have the means to comfort and restore the tutor minister."
translations.idiomatic: May Your Majesty weigh the general feeling and offer some consolation to the man who was once your tutor."

sentences.zh: 上從林言，下詔賜丹爵關內侯，食邑三百戶。

translations.literal: The sovereign followed Lin's words; he issued an edict granting Dan rank Marquis Within the Passes, with fief income of three hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor accepted Tang Lin's advice and issued an edict making Dan marquis within the passes with three hundred taxable households.

sentences.zh: 丹既免數月，上用朱博議，尊傅太后為皇太太后，丁后為帝太后，與太皇太后及皇太后同尊，又為共皇立廟京師，儀如孝元皇帝。

translations.literal: Several months after Dan was dismissed, the sovereign used Zhu Bo's plan, honoring Empress Dowager Fu as Grand Grand Empress Dowager and Empress Ding as Imperial Empress Dowager, with the same veneration as the Grand Empress Dowager and Empress Dowager; also for the Gong Emperor he established a temple in the capital, ritual like Emperor Xiaoyuan.
translations.idiomatic: Months after Dan's removal the emperor adopted Zhu Bo's advice: Empress Dowager Fu became grand grand empress dowager, Empress Ding became imperial empress dowager, coequal in honor with the grand empress dowager and the empress dowager; a temple to Emperor Gong was raised in the capital on the model of Emperor Yuan's rites.

sentences.zh: 博遷為丞相，復與御史大夫趙玄奏言：「前高昌侯宏首建尊號之議，而為丹所劾奏，免為庶人。

translations.literal: Bo was transferred to be Chancellor; again together with Imperial Counselor Zhao Xuan he submitted saying: "Formerly Marquis of Gaochang Hong first raised the plan of honorific titles, yet was impeached by Dan and dismissed to be a commoner.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Bo became chancellor and jointly with Imperial Counselor Zhao Xuan submitted: "Marquis of Gaochang Dong Hong was the first to urge the new honorific titles, yet Shi Dan impeached him and had him reduced to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 時天下衰麤，委政於丹。

translations.literal: At the time all under Heaven declined and was coarse; they entrusted government to Dan.
translations.idiomatic: The realm was in turmoil and the decision rested with Dan.

sentences.zh: 丹不深惟褒廣尊親之義而妄稱說，抑貶尊號，虧損孝道，不忠莫大焉。

translations.literal: Dan did not deeply consider the meaning of praising and broadening honored kin but recklessly spoke; he suppressed and demoted honorific titles, injuring and diminishing filial piety—no disloyalty is greater.
translations.idiomatic: Dan gave no thought to exalting the emperor's kin but spread reckless talk, belittling the new titles and wounding filial piety—there is no greater disloyalty.

sentences.zh: 陛下聖仁，昭然定尊號，宏以忠孝復封高昌侯。

translations.literal: Your Majesty is sage and humane; clearly you fixed the honorific titles; Hong by loyalty and filial piety was again enfeoffed Marquis of Gaochang.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty in sage clarity settled the titles and restored Hong as marquis of Gaochang for his loyalty and filial devotion.

sentences.zh: 丹惡逆暴著，雖蒙赦令，不宜有爵邑，請免為庶人。」

translations.literal: Dan's evil and rebellion are violently manifest; although he received an amnesty edict, it is not fitting that he have rank and fief; your servants request dismissal to commoner."
translations.idiomatic: Dan's malice stands plain for all to see; though pardoned from criminal trial, he should not keep title or fief—reduce him to commoner status."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was permitted.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved the memorial.

sentences.zh: 丹於是廢歸鄉里者數年。

translations.literal: Dan thereupon was cast off and returned to his village district for several years.
translations.idiomatic: Dan was then stripped of office and lived in his home commandery for several years.

sentences.zh: 平帝即位，新都侯王莽白太皇太后發掘傅太后、丁太后冢，奪其璽綬，更以民葬之，定陶隳廢共皇廟。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ping ascended, Marquis of Xindu Wang Mang reported to the Grand Empress Dowager to dig up Empress Dowager Fu's and Empress Dowager Ding's tombs, seize their seals and ribbons, again bury them with commoner burial; Dingtao was destroyed and the Gong Emperor's temple abolished.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ping came to the throne, Marquis of Xindu Wang Mang persuaded the grand empress dowager to open the tombs of the empresses dowager Fu and Ding, strip their imperial seals, and rebury them as commoners; the shrines at Dingtao to Emperor Gong were torn down.

sentences.zh: 諸造議泠褒、段猶等皆徙合浦，復免高昌侯宏為庶人。

translations.literal: The various who made the plans Ling Bao, Duan You, and others were all banished to Hepu; again Marquis of Gaochang Hong was dismissed to be a commoner.
translations.idiomatic: The architects of the policy—Ling Bao, Duan You, and their ilk—were banished to Hepu, and Marquis of Gaochang Dong Hong was again reduced to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 徵丹詣公車，賜爵關內侯，食故邑。

translations.literal: Dan was summoned to the Public Carriage Gate; he was granted rank Marquis Within the Passes, eating his former fief.
translations.idiomatic: Dan was summoned to the public carriage gate, made marquis within the passes, and given his old stipendiary estate back.

sentences.zh: 數月，太皇太后詔大司徒、大司空曰：「夫褒有德，賞元功，先聖之制，百王不易之道也。

translations.literal: After several months the Grand Empress Dowager edicted the Grand Minister of Education and Grand Minister of Works, saying: "As for praising those with virtue and rewarding those with first merit, it is the former sages' system, the hundred kings' unchanging way.
translations.idiomatic: Months later the grand empress dowager addressed the grand minister of education and grand minister of works: "To honor the virtuous and reward founding merit is the teaching of the ancient sages and the constant rule of every true king.

sentences.zh: 故定陶太后造稱僭號，甚悖義理。

translations.literal: Therefore the Dingtao Empress Dowager forged titles and usurped style—greatly contrary to righteousness and principle.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager of Dingtao arrogated titles that violated every principle of right.

sentences.zh: 關內侯師丹端誠於國，不顧患難，執忠節，據聖法，分明尊卑之制，確然有柱石之固，臨大節而不可奪，可謂社稷之臣矣。

translations.literal: Marquis Within the Passes Shi Dan was upright and sincere toward the state; he did not look to hardship and peril; he grasped loyal integrity; he relied on sage law; he clarified the system of honored and humble; firmly he had pillar-and-stone solidity; facing great constancy he could not be snatched away—he may be called a minister of the altars of soil and grain.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis within the passes Shi Dan served the state with single-minded loyalty, heedless of personal danger; he upheld the statutes, drew the line between proper rank high and low, and stood like a cornerstone that no crisis could shake—a true pillar of the dynasty.

sentences.zh: 有司條奏邪臣建定稱號者已放退，而丹功賞未加，殆繆乎先賞後罰之義，非所以章有德報厥功也。

translations.literal: The responsible offices in items memorialized that the evil ministers who established and fixed titles have already been banished and retired, yet Dan's merit and reward have not been added—probably erring from the meaning of first reward then punish; it is not the means to display those with virtue and repay their merit.
translations.idiomatic: The ministries have already banished the wicked ministers who devised those titles, yet Dan has received no reward for his service—that inverts the principle of rewarding merit before inflicting punishment and fails to make manifest his virtue.

sentences.zh: 其以厚丘之中鄉戶二千一百封丹為義陽侯。」

translations.literal: Thereby with Houqiu's Zhongxiang, households 2,100, enfeoff Dan as Marquis of Yiyang."
translations.idiomatic: Enfeoff Dan as marquis of Yiyang with the two thousand one hundred households of Zhong township in Houqiu commandery."

sentences.zh: 月餘薨，諡曰節侯。

translations.literal: After more than a month he died; his posthumous title was Marquis Jie.
translations.idiomatic: He died a little over a month later and was posthumously titled Marquis of Integrity.

sentences.zh: 子業嗣，王莽敗乃絕。

translations.literal: His son Ye succeeded; when Wang Mang was defeated it then ended.
translations.idiomatic: His son Ye inherited the title; the line ended when Wang Mang fell.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：何武之舉，王嘉之爭，師丹之議，考其禍福，乃效於後。

translations.literal: The eulogy says: He Wu's recommendations, Wang Jia's contention, Shi Dan's disputation—in examining their disaster and blessing, the effect was afterward.
translations.idiomatic: The historian's judgment: He Wu's nominations, Wang Jia's remonstrance, Shi Dan's policy debates—study their outcomes, and the sequel proves the point.

sentences.zh: 當王莽之作，外內咸服，董賢之愛，疑於親戚，武、嘉區區，以一蕢障江河，用沒其身。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang arose, outer and inner all submitted; Dong Xian's favor was suspected alongside kin; Wu and Jia in their petty way, with one basket-screen blocking river and stream, thereby destroyed their persons.
translations.idiomatic: While Wang Mang won obedience inside and out, and Dong Xian's favor rivaled kinship itself, Wu and Jia stood their ground like men trying to dam a river with a reed mat—and paid with their lives.

sentences.zh: 丹與董宏更受賞罰，哀哉！

translations.literal: Dan and Dong Hong again received reward and punishment—alas!
translations.idiomatic: Shi Dan and Dong Hong alike knew reward and ruin—how bitter a fate!

sentences.zh: 故曰「依世則廢道，違俗則免殆」，此古人所以難受爵位者也。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: "Following the generation then the Way is abandoned; violating the custom then one escapes peril"—this is why the ancients found it hard to receive rank and position.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: "Heed the times and you abandon the right; defy fashion and you may escape ruin"—that is why the ancients found high office so hard to bear.
